channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nigeria D’Tigress Come From Behind To Beat Philippines 101-84 In FIBA Qualifier
item.description: D’Tigress, Naija women basketball team, don show serious spirit as dem beat Philippines 101-84 for Group A match of 2026 FIBA Women’s Basketball World Cup Qualifying Tournament wey dey hold for Villeu...

item.title: Opposition raises concerns over Election Commission’s impartiality before assembly election dates announcement
item.description: The Election Commission of India is set to announce the schedule for the upcoming assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal. These states’ assembly terms are ending ...

item.title: Omotola Confirms She and Genevieve Still Dey Friends
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress, Omotola Jalade Ekeinde don clear air about her relationship with fellow actress, Genevieve Nnaji. Some people dey believe say dem no dey on good terms again, especially as d...

item.title: Feminism's Ongoing Struggle and Achievements
item.description: Feminism is far from finished, despite frequent claims of its demise. Rebecca Solnit emphasises that declaring feminism dead is based on unrealistic expectations and selective measures of success, suc...

item.title: Ontario Mayor Orders Removal of Two Tigers from Residential Property
item.description: In Wainfleet, Ontario, the owner of two sub-adult tigers has been ordered to remove them from his residential property. Zohaib Masood had kept the tigers on a former mushroom farm since last fall and ...

item.title: Northern Star Resources faces challenges as it transitions from junior explorer to major miner
item.description: Northern Star Resources, once a small gold explorer in the early 2010s, has grown into one of Australia’s leading gold producers. Investors who backed the company under its early 'Three Wise Men' stra...

item.title: Ongoing Conflict in the Middle East: US, Israel, and Iran Engage in Strikes and Diplomatic Maneuvers
item.description: The Middle East is experiencing a surge in military tensions as the conflict between Iran, the United States, and Israel continues. US President Donald Trump stated he is not ready to negotiate a deal...

item.title: At least 20 security personnel killed in ambush by suspected bandits in Plateau
item.description: For Plateau State, wahala just dey full ground as at least 20 security personnel and local vigilantes don die after suspected bandits ambushed dem on Friday for Garga area, Kanam Local Government. Acc...

item.title: Punjab Govt Announces Legal Steps Against Alleged False Claims on CM's Official Jet Usage
item.description: Senior Minister Marriyum Aurangzeb announced on Sunday that the Punjab government will pursue legal action against individuals and platforms spreading what it describes as a deliberate malicious campa...

item.title: Former Illinois Congress Members Lead Primaries with Heavy Outside Funding
item.description: In Illinois Democratic primaries, the party's push for younger leaders faces challenges from established politicians backed by millions in special interest money. Former Rep. Jesse Jackson Jr., who le...

item.title: Using Wastewater Treatment Plants to Turn Food Waste into Energy and Fertilizer
item.description: Across the United States, roughly 97 million metric tons of food waste are thrown away each year, with about 37 million tons ending up buried in landfills. When this organic matter decomposes without ...

item.title: Authorities confiscate P10.2 million worth of shabu in two Zamboanga City operations
item.description: Agents from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) Region 9, together with the Zamboanga City Police Office, successfully conducted two separate anti-drug entrapment operations on March 14, 202...

item.title: Dispute Erupts During Vihiga Pastor’s Funeral Between Family and Church Leaders
item.description: A funeral in Chavakali, Vihiga County, intended to honour the late Reverend Hudson Jumba Mang’ang’a, turned chaotic after disagreements between his family and church officials. Rev Jumba had served th...

item.title: Prime Minister directs full investigation into hospital medicine supply and distribution
item.description: PM Mwigulu Nchemba ametoa maagizo ya kufanya uchunguzi kamili juu ya mfumo wa usambazaji na ugawaji wa dawa kwenye hospitali na vituo vya afya kote Tanzania. Alitoa maagizo hayo siku ya Jumapili, Mach...

item.title: Three Iranian Women's Football Team Members Return to Iran After Seeking Asylum in Australia
item.description: Three members of Iran's women's national football team who had sought asylum in Australia have reversed their decision and opted to return to Iran. Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke confirmed that the ...

item.title: Understanding ‘Job Hugging’: Staying in a Job You’re Unsatisfied With
item.description: Many Canadians find themselves feeling dissatisfied with their current employment but feel unable to leave due to financial, social, or career-related constraints. This phenomenon, sometimes called 'j...

item.title: Retired police officer and former aide of Mayor Isko Moreno shot dead by riding-in-tandem gunmen in Tondo
item.description: A retired police officer who previously served as the security escort of former Manila mayor Isko Moreno was shot dead by unidentified riding-in-tandem gunmen in Tondo, Manila, on Sunday, March 15, 20...

item.title: Islamabad Airport Flight Operations to Continue Normally on 16th March
item.description: The Pakistan Airport Authority (PAA) has clarified that the previously issued Notam, which announced a temporary suspension of flights at Islamabad International Airport on 16th March from 08:30am to ...

item.title: Flooding affects Longreach in western Queensland
item.description: Heavy rainfall has caused water to seep into the outback town of Longreach, located in western Queensland. Residents are experiencing disruptions as floodwaters impact roads, properties, and daily rou...

item.title: Government allocates funds to construct and renovate PCCB offices across Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government has earmarked 3.82 billion Tanzanian shillings for the 2025/2026 financial year to construct and renovate offices for the Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB)....
